189â†’ * ```
190â†’ * <!--- TEST -->
191â†’ *
192â†’ * Note that the resulting flow does not emit anything as long as the original flow emits
193â†’ * items faster than every [timeout] unit.
194â†’ *
195â†’ * @param timeout [T] is the emitted value and the return value is timeout in [Duration].
196â†’ */
197â†’@FlowPreview
198â†’@JvmName("debounceDuration")
199â†’@OptIn(kotlin.experimental.ExperimentalTypeInference::class)
200â†’@OverloadResolutionByLambdaReturnType
201â†’public fun <T> Flow<T>.debounce(timeout: (T) -> Duration): Flow<T> =
202â†’    debounceInternal { emittedItem ->
203â†’        timeout(emittedItem).toDelayMillis()
204â†’    }
205â†’
206â†’private fun <T> Flow<T>.debounceInternal(timeoutMillisSelector: (T) -> Long): Flow<T> =
207â†’    scopedFlow { downstream ->
208â†’        // Produce the values using the default (rendezvous) channel
209â†’        val values = produce {
210â†’            collect { value -> send(value ?: NULL) }
211â†’        }
212â†’        // Now consume the values
213â†’        var lastValue: Any? = null
214â†’        while (lastValue !== DONE) {
215â†’            var timeoutMillis = 0L // will be always computed when lastValue != null
216â†’            // Compute timeout for this value
217â†’            if (lastValue != null) {
218â†’                timeoutMillis = timeoutMillisSelector(NULL.unbox(lastValue))
219â†’                require(timeoutMillis >= 0L) { "Debounce timeout should not be negative" }
220â†’                if (timeoutMillis == 0L) {
221â†’                    downstream.emit(NULL.unbox(lastValue))
222â†’                    lastValue = null // Consume the value
223â†’                }
224â†’            }
225â†’            // assert invariant: lastValue != null implies timeoutMillis > 0
226â†’            assert { lastValue == null || timeoutMillis > 0 }
227â†’            // wait for the next value with timeout
228â†’            select<Unit> {
229â†’                // Set timeout when lastValue exists and is not consumed yet
230â†’                if (lastValue != null) {
231â†’                    onTimeout(timeoutMillis) {
232â†’                        downstream.emit(NULL.unbox(lastValue))
233â†’                        lastValue = null // Consume the value
234â†’                    }
235â†’                }
236â†’                values.onReceiveCatching { value ->
237â†’                    value
238â†’                        .onSuccess { lastValue = it }
239â†’                        .onFailure {
240â†’                            it?.let { throw it }
241â†’                            // If closed normally, emit the latest value
242â†’                            if (lastValue != null) downstream.emit(NULL.unbox(lastValue))
243â†’                            lastValue = DONE
244â†’                        }
245â†’                }
246â†’            }
247â†’        }
248â†’    }
249â†’
250â†’/**
251â†’ * Returns a flow that emits only the latest value emitted by the original flow during the given sampling [period][periodMillis].
252â†’ *
253â†’ * Example:
254â†’ *
255â†’ * ```kotlin
256â†’ * flow {
257â†’ *     repeat(10) {
258â†’ *         emit(it)
259â†’ *         delay(110)
260â†’ *     }
261â†’ * }.sample(200)
262â†’ * ```
263â†’ * <!--- KNIT example-delay-03.kt -->
264â†’ *
265â†’ * produces the following emissions
266â†’ *
267â†’ * ```text
268â†’ * 1, 3, 5, 7, 9
269â†’ * ```
270â†’ * <!--- TEST -->
271â†’ *
272â†’ * Note that the latest element is not emitted if it does not fit into the sampling window.
273â†’ */
274â†’@FlowPreview
275â†’public fun <T> Flow<T>.sample(periodMillis: Long): Flow<T> {
276â†’    require(periodMillis > 0) { "Sample period should be positive" }
277â†’    return scopedFlow { downstream ->
278â†’        val values = produce(capacity = Channel.CONFLATED) {
279â†’            collect { value -> send(value ?: NULL) }
280â†’        }
281â†’        var lastValue: Any? = null
282â†’        val ticker = fixedPeriodTicker(periodMillis)
283â†’        while (lastValue !== DONE) {
284â†’            select<Unit> {
285â†’                values.onReceiveCatching { result ->
286â†’                    result
287â†’                        .onSuccess { lastValue = it }
288â†’                        .onFailure {
289â†’                            it?.let { throw it }
290â†’                            ticker.cancel(ChildCancelledException())
291â†’                            lastValue = DONE
292â†’                        }
293â†’                }
294â†’
295â†’                // todo: shall be start sampling only when an element arrives or sample aways as here?
296â†’                ticker.onReceive {
297â†’                    val value = lastValue ?: return@onReceive
298â†’                    lastValue = null // Consume the value
299â†’                    downstream.emit(NULL.unbox(value))
300â†’                }
301â†’            }
302â†’        }
303â†’    }
304â†’}

306â†’/*
307â†’ * TODO this design (and design of the corresponding operator) depends on #540
308â†’ */
309â†’internal fun CoroutineScope.fixedPeriodTicker(
310â†’    delayMillis: Long,
311â†’    initialDelayMillis: Long = delayMillis
312â†’): ReceiveChannel<Unit> {
313â†’    require(delayMillis >= 0) { "Expected non-negative delay, but has $delayMillis ms" }
314â†’    require(initialDelayMillis >= 0) { "Expected non-negative initial delay, but has $initialDelayMillis ms" }
315â†’    return produce(capacity = 0) {
316â†’        delay(initialDelayMillis)
317â†’        while (true) {
318â†’            channel.send(Unit)
319â†’            delay(delayMillis)
320â†’        }
321â†’    }
322â†’}

334â†’ *     }
335â†’ * }.sample(200.milliseconds)

348â†’@FlowPreview

351â†’/**
352â†’ * Returns a flow that will emit a [TimeoutCancellationException] if the upstream doesn't emit an item within the given time.
353â†’ *
354â†’ * Example:
355â†’ *
356â†’ * ```kotlin
357â†’ * flow {
358â†’ *     emit(1)
359â†’ *     delay(100)
360â†’ *     emit(2)
361â†’ *     delay(100)
362â†’ *     emit(3)
363â†’ *     delay(1000)
364â†’ *     emit(4)
365â†’ * }.timeout(100.milliseconds).catch { exception ->
366â†’ *     if (exception is TimeoutCancellationException) {
367â†’ *         // Catch the TimeoutCancellationException emitted above.
368â†’ *         // Emit desired item on timeout.
369â†’ *         emit(-1)
370â†’ *     } else {
371â†’ *         // Throw other exceptions.
372â†’ *         throw exception
373â†’ *     }
374â†’ * }.onEach {
375â†’ *     delay(300) // This will not cause a timeout
376â†’ * }
377â†’ * ```
378â†’ * <!--- KNIT example-timeout-duration-01.kt -->
379â†’ *
380â†’ * produces the following emissions
381â†’ *
382â†’ * ```text
383â†’ * 1, 2, 3, -1
384â†’ * ```
385â†’ * <!--- TEST -->
386â†’ *
387â†’ * Note that delaying on the downstream doesn't trigger the timeout.
388â†’ *
389â†’ * @param timeout Timeout duration. If non-positive, the flow is timed out immediately
390â†’ */
391â†’@FlowPreview
392â†’public fun <T> Flow<T>.timeout(
393â†’    timeout: Duration
394â†’): Flow<T> = timeoutInternal(timeout)
395â†’
396â†’private fun <T> Flow<T>.timeoutInternal(
397â†’    timeout: Duration
398â†’): Flow<T> = scopedFlow { downStream ->
399â†’    if (timeout <= Duration.ZERO) throw TimeoutCancellationException("Timed out immediately")
400â†’    val values = buffer(Channel.RENDEZVOUS).produceIn(this)
401â†’    whileSelect {
402â†’        values.onReceiveCatching { value ->
403â†’            value.onSuccess {
404â†’                downStream.emit(it)
405â†’            }.onClosed {
406â†’                return@onReceiveCatching false
407â†’            }
408â†’            return@onReceiveCatching true
409â†’        }
410â†’        onTimeout(timeout) {
411â†’            throw TimeoutCancellationException("Timed out waiting for $timeout")
412â†’        }
413â†’    }
414â†’}

 17â†’/**
 18â†’ * Waits for the result of multiple suspending functions simultaneously, which are specified using _clauses_
 19â†’ * in the [builder] scope of this select invocation. The caller is suspended until one of the clauses
 20â†’ * is either _selected_ or _fails_.
 21â†’ *
 22â†’ * At most one clause is *atomically* selected and its block is executed. The result of the selected clause
 23â†’ * becomes the result of the select. If any clause _fails_, then the select invocation produces the
 24â†’ * corresponding exception. No clause is selected in this case.
 25â†’ *
 26â†’ * This select function is _biased_ to the first clause. When multiple clauses can be selected at the same time,
 27â†’ * the first one of them gets priority. Use [selectUnbiased] for an unbiased (randomized) selection among
 28â†’ * the clauses.
 29â†’
 30â†’ * There is no `default` clause for select expression. Instead, each selectable suspending function has the
 31â†’ * corresponding non-suspending version that can be used with a regular `when` expression to select one
 32â†’ * of the alternatives or to perform the default (`else`) action if none of them can be immediately selected.
 33â†’ *
 34â†’ * ### List of supported select methods
 35â†’ *
 36â†’ * | **Receiver**     | **Suspending function**                           | **Select clause**
 37â†’ * | ---------------- | ---------------------------------------------     | -----------------------------------------------------
 38â†’ * | [Job]            | [join][Job.join]                                  | [onJoin][Job.onJoin]
 39â†’ * | [Deferred]       | [await][Deferred.await]                           | [onAwait][Deferred.onAwait]
 40â†’ * | [SendChannel]    | [send][SendChannel.send]                          | [onSend][SendChannel.onSend]
 41â†’ * | [ReceiveChannel] | [receive][ReceiveChannel.receive]                 | [onReceive][ReceiveChannel.onReceive]
 42â†’ * | [ReceiveChannel] | [receiveCatching][ReceiveChannel.receiveCatching] | [onReceiveCatching][ReceiveChannel.onReceiveCatching]
 43â†’ * | none             | [delay]                                           | [onTimeout][SelectBuilder.onTimeout]
 44â†’ *
 45â†’ * This suspending function is cancellable. If the [Job] of the current coroutine is cancelled or completed while this
 46â†’ * function is suspended, this function immediately resumes with [CancellationException].
 47â†’ * There is a **prompt cancellation guarantee**. If the job was cancelled while this function was
 48â†’ * suspended, it will not resume successfully. See [suspendCancellableCoroutine] documentation for low-level details.
 49â†’ *
 50â†’ * Note that this function does not check for cancellation when it is not suspended.
 51â†’ * Use [yield] or [CoroutineScope.isActive] to periodically check for cancellation in tight loops if needed.
 52â†’ */
 53â†’@OptIn(ExperimentalContracts::class)
 54â†’public suspend inline fun <R> select(crossinline builder: SelectBuilder<R>.() -> Unit): R {
 55â†’    contract {
 56â†’        callsInPlace(builder, InvocationKind.EXACTLY_ONCE)
 57â†’    }
 58â†’    return SelectImplementation<R>(coroutineContext).run {
 59â†’        builder(this)
 60â†’        // TAIL-CALL OPTIMIZATION: the only
 61â†’        // suspend call is at the last position.
 62â†’        doSelect()
 63â†’    }
 64â†’}
 65â†’
 66â†’/**
 67â†’ * Scope for [select] invocation.
 68â†’ *
 69â†’ * An instance of [SelectBuilder] can only be retrieved as a receiver of a [select] block call,
 70â†’ * and it is only valid during the registration phase of the select builder.
 71â†’ * Any uses outside it lead to unspecified behaviour and are prohibited.
 72â†’ *
 73â†’ * The general rule of thumb is that instances of this type should always be used
 74â†’ * implicitly and there shouldn't be any signatures mentioning this type,
 75â†’ * whether explicitly (e.g. function signature) or implicitly (e.g. inferred `val` type).
 76â†’ */
 77â†’public sealed interface SelectBuilder<in R> {
 78â†’    /**
 79â†’     * Registers a clause in this [select] expression without additional parameters that does not select any value.
 80â†’     */
 81â†’    public operator fun SelectClause0.invoke(block: suspend () -> R)
 82â†’
 83â†’    /**
 84â†’     * Registers clause in this [select] expression without additional parameters that selects value of type [Q].
 85â†’     */
 86â†’    public operator fun <Q> SelectClause1<Q>.invoke(block: suspend (Q) -> R)
 87â†’
 88â†’    /**
 89â†’     * Registers clause in this [select] expression with additional parameter of type [P] that selects value of type [Q].
 90â†’     */
 91â†’    public operator fun <P, Q> SelectClause2<P, Q>.invoke(param: P, block: suspend (Q) -> R)
 92â†’
 93â†’    /**
 94â†’     * Registers clause in this [select] expression with additional nullable parameter of type [P]
 95â†’     * with the `null` value for this parameter that selects value of type [Q].
 96â†’     */
 97â†’    public operator fun <P, Q> SelectClause2<P?, Q>.invoke(block: suspend (Q) -> R): Unit = invoke(null, block)
 98â†’
 99â†’    /**
100â†’     * Clause that selects the given [block] after a specified timeout passes.
101â†’     * If timeout is negative or zero, [block] is selected immediately.
102â†’     *
103â†’     * **Note: This is an experimental api.** It may be replaced with light-weight timer/timeout channels in the future.
104â†’     *
105â†’     * @param timeMillis timeout time in milliseconds.
106â†’     */
107â†’    @ExperimentalCoroutinesApi
108â†’    @Suppress("INVISIBLE_REFERENCE", "INVISIBLE_MEMBER")
109â†’    @LowPriorityInOverloadResolution
110â†’    @Deprecated(
111â†’        message = "Replaced with the same extension function",
112â†’        level = DeprecationLevel.ERROR, replaceWith = ReplaceWith(expression = "onTimeout", imports = ["kotlinx.coroutines.selects.onTimeout"])
113â†’    ) // Since 1.7.0, was experimental
114â†’    public fun onTimeout(timeMillis: Long, block: suspend () -> R): Unit = onTimeout(timeMillis, block)
115â†’}
116â†’
117â†’/**
118â†’ * Each [select] clause is specified with:
119â†’ * 1) the [object of this clause][clauseObject],
120â†’ *    such as the channel instance for [SendChannel.onSend];
121â†’ * 2) the function that specifies how this clause
122â†’ *    should be registered in the object above;
123â†’ * 3) the function that modifies the internal result
124â†’ *    (passed via [SelectInstance.trySelect] or
125â†’ *    [SelectInstance.selectInRegistrationPhase])
126â†’ *    to the argument of the user-specified block.
127â†’ * 4) the function that specifies how the internal result provided via
128â†’ *    [SelectInstance.trySelect] or [SelectInstance.selectInRegistrationPhase]
129â†’ *    should be processed in case of this `select` cancellation while dispatching.
130â†’ */
131â†’@InternalCoroutinesApi
132â†’public sealed interface SelectClause {
133â†’    public val clauseObject: Any
134â†’    public val regFunc: RegistrationFunction
135â†’    public val processResFunc: ProcessResultFunction
136â†’    public val onCancellationConstructor: OnCancellationConstructor?
137â†’}
138â†’
139â†’/**
140â†’ * The registration function specifies how the `select` instance should be registered into
141â†’ * the specified clause object. In case of channels, the registration logic
142â†’ * coincides with the plain `send/receive` operation with the only difference that
143â†’ * the `select` instance is stored as a waiter instead of continuation.
144â†’ */
145â†’@InternalCoroutinesApi
146â†’public typealias RegistrationFunction = (clauseObject: Any, select: SelectInstance<*>, param: Any?) -> Unit
147â†’
148â†’/**
149â†’ * This function specifies how the _internal_ result, provided via [SelectInstance.selectInRegistrationPhase]
150â†’ * or [SelectInstance.trySelect] should be processed. For example, both [ReceiveChannel.onReceive] and
151â†’ * [ReceiveChannel.onReceiveCatching] clauses perform exactly the same synchronization logic,
152â†’ * but differ when the channel has been discovered in the closed or cancelled state.
153â†’ */
154â†’@InternalCoroutinesApi
155â†’public typealias ProcessResultFunction = (clauseObject: Any, param: Any?, clauseResult: Any?) -> Any?
156â†’
157â†’/**
158â†’ * This function specifies how the internal result, provided via [SelectInstance.trySelect]
159â†’ * or [SelectInstance.selectInRegistrationPhase], should be processed in case of this `select`
160â†’ * cancellation while dispatching. Unfortunately, we cannot pass this function only in [SelectInstance.trySelect],
161â†’ * as [SelectInstance.selectInRegistrationPhase] can be called when the coroutine is already cancelled.
162â†’ */
163â†’@InternalCoroutinesApi
164â†’public typealias OnCancellationConstructor = (select: SelectInstance<*>, param: Any?, internalResult: Any?) -> (Throwable) -> Unit
165â†’

204â†’/**
205â†’ * Internal representation of `select` instance.
206â†’ *
207â†’ * @suppress **This is unstable API, and it is subject to change.**
208â†’ */
209â†’@InternalCoroutinesApi
210â†’public sealed interface SelectInstance<in R> {
211â†’    /**
212â†’     * The context of the coroutine that is performing this `select` operation.
213â†’     */
214â†’    public val context: CoroutineContext
215â†’
216â†’    /**
217â†’     * This function should be called by other operations,
218â†’     * which are trying to perform a rendezvous with this `select`.
219â†’     * Returns `true` if the rendezvous succeeds, `false` otherwise.
220â†’     *
221â†’     * Note that according to the current implementation, a rendezvous attempt can fail
222â†’     * when either another clause is already selected or this `select` is still in
223â†’     * REGISTRATION phase. To distinguish the reasons, [SelectImplementation.trySelectDetailed]
224â†’     * function can be used instead.
225â†’     */
226â†’    public fun trySelect(clauseObject: Any, result: Any?): Boolean
227â†’
228â†’    /**
229â†’     * When this `select` instance is stored as a waiter, the specified [handle][disposableHandle]
230â†’     * defines how the stored `select` should be removed in case of cancellation or another clause selection.
231â†’     */
232â†’    public fun disposeOnCompletion(disposableHandle: DisposableHandle)
233â†’
234â†’    /**
235â†’     * When a clause becomes selected during registration, the corresponding internal result
236â†’     * (which is further passed to the clause's [ProcessResultFunction]) should be provided
237â†’     * via this function. After that, other clause registrations are ignored and [trySelect] fails.
238â†’     */
239â†’    public fun selectInRegistrationPhase(internalResult: Any?)
240â†’}
241â†’internal interface SelectInstanceInternal<R>: SelectInstance<R>, Waiter
242â†’
243â†’@PublishedApi
244â†’internal open class SelectImplementation<R>(
245â†’    override val context: CoroutineContext
246â†’) : CancelHandler(), SelectBuilder<R>, SelectInstanceInternal<R> {
247â†’
248â†’    /**
249â†’     * Essentially, the `select` operation is split into three phases: REGISTRATION, WAITING, and COMPLETION.
250â†’     *
251â†’     * == Phase 1: REGISTRATION ==
252â†’     * In the first REGISTRATION phase, the user-specified [SelectBuilder] is applied, and all the listed clauses
253â†’     * are registered via the provided [registration functions][SelectClause.regFunc]. Intuitively, `select` clause
254â†’     * registration is similar to the plain blocking operation, with the only difference that this [SelectInstance]
255â†’     * is stored as a waiter instead of continuation, and [SelectInstance.trySelect] is used to make a rendezvous.
256â†’     * Also, when registering, it is possible for the operation to complete immediately, without waiting. In this case,
257â†’     * [SelectInstance.selectInRegistrationPhase] should be used. Otherwise, when no rendezvous happens and this `select`
258â†’     * instance is stored as a waiter, a completion handler for the registering clause should be specified via
259â†’     * [SelectInstance.disposeOnCompletion]; this handler specifies how to remove this `select` instance from the
260â†’     * clause object when another clause becomes selected or the operation cancels.
261â†’     *
262â†’     * After a clause registration is completed, another coroutine can attempt to make a rendezvous with this `select`.
263â†’     * However, to resolve a race between clauses registration and [SelectInstance.trySelect], the latter fails when
264â†’     * this `select` is still in REGISTRATION phase. Thus, the corresponding clause has to be registered again.
265â†’     *
266â†’     * In this phase, the `state` field stores either a special [STATE_REG] marker or
267â†’     * a list of clauses to be re-registered due to failed rendezvous attempts.
268â†’     *
269â†’     * == Phase 2: WAITING ==
270â†’     * If no rendezvous happens in REGISTRATION phase, the `select` operation moves to WAITING one and suspends until
271â†’     * [SelectInstance.trySelect] is called. Also, when waiting, this `select` can be cancelled. In the latter case,
272â†’     * further [SelectInstance.trySelect] attempts fail, and all the completion handlers, specified via
273â†’     * [SelectInstance.disposeOnCompletion], are invoked to remove this `select` instance from the corresponding
274â†’     * clause objects.
275â†’     *
276â†’     * In this phase, the `state` field stores either the continuation to be later resumed or a special `Cancelled`
277â†’     * object (with the cancellation cause inside) when this `select` becomes cancelled.
278â†’     *
279â†’     * == Phase 3: COMPLETION ==
280â†’     * Once a rendezvous happens either in REGISTRATION phase (via [SelectInstance.selectInRegistrationPhase]) or
281â†’     * in WAITING phase (via [SelectInstance.trySelect]), this `select` moves to the final `COMPLETION` phase.
282â†’     * First, the provided internal result is processed via the [ProcessResultFunction] of the selected clause;
283â†’     * it returns the argument for the user-specified block or throws an exception (see [SendChannel.onSend] as
284â†’     * an example). After that, this `select` should be removed from all other clause objects by calling the
285â†’     * corresponding [DisposableHandle]-s, provided via [SelectInstance.disposeOnCompletion] during registration.
286â†’     * At the end, the user-specified block is called and this `select` finishes.
287â†’     *
288â†’     * In this phase, once a rendezvous is happened, the `state` field stores the corresponding clause.
289â†’     * After that, it moves to [STATE_COMPLETED] to avoid memory leaks.
290â†’     *
291â†’     *
292â†’     *
293â†’     * The state machine is listed below:
294â†’     *
295â†’     *            REGISTRATION PHASE                   WAITING PHASE             COMPLETION PHASE
296â†’     *       âŚ˘âŚ˘âŚ˘âŚ˘âŚ˘âŚ˘âŚ˘âŚ˘âŚ˘âŚ˘âŚ˘âŚ˘âŚ˘âŚ˘âŚ˘âŚ˘âŚ˘âŚ˘âŚ˘âŚ˘             âŚ˘âŚ˘âŚ˘âŚ˘âŚ˘âŚ˘âŚ˘âŚ˘âŚ˘âŚ˘âŚ˘âŚ˘         âŚ˘âŚ˘âŚ˘âŚ˘âŚ˘âŚ˘âŚ˘âŚ˘âŚ˘âŚ˘âŚ˘âŚ˘âŚ˘âŚ˘
297â†’     *
298â†’     *                                                 +-----------+                 +-----------+
299â†’     *                                                 | CANCELLED |                 | COMPLETED |
300â†’     *                                                 +-----------+                 +-----------+
301â†’     *                                                       ^                             ^
302â†’     *     INITIAL STATE                                     |                             | this `select`
303â†’     *     ------------+                                     |  cancelled                  | is completed
304â†’     *                  \                                    |                             |
305â†’     *                   +=============+     move to     +------+    successful   +------------+
306â†’     *                +--|  STATE_REG  |---------------> | cont |-----------------| ClauseData |
307â†’     *                |  +=============+  WAITING phase  +------+  trySelect(..)  +------------+
308â†’     *                |    ^     |                                                       ^
309â†’     *                |    |     |    some clause has been selected during registration  |
310â†’     *         add a  |    |     +-------------------------------------------------------+
311â†’     *   clause to be |    |                                                             |
312â†’     *  re-registered |    | re-register                   some clause has been selected |
313â†’     *                |    | clauses                     during registration while there |
314â†’     *                v    |                            are clauses to be re-registered; |
315â†’     *          +------------------+                                   ignore the latter |
316â†’     *       +--| List<ClauseData> |-----------------------------------------------------+
317â†’     *       |  +------------------+
318â†’     *       |            ^
319â†’     *       |            |  add one more clause
320â†’     *       |            |  for re-registration
321â†’     *       +------------+
322â†’     *
323â†’     * One of the most valuable benefits of this `select` design is that it allows processing clauses
324â†’     * in a way similar to plain operations, such as `send` or `receive` on channels. The only difference
325â†’     * is that instead of continuation, the operation should store the provided `select` instance object.
326â†’     * Thus, this design makes it possible to support the `select` expression for any blocking data structure
327â†’     * in Kotlin Coroutines.
328â†’     *
329â†’     * It is worth mentioning that the algorithm above provides "obstruction-freedom" non-blocking guarantee
330â†’     * instead of the standard "lock-freedom" to avoid using heavy descriptors. In practice, this relaxation
331â†’     * does not make significant difference. However, it is vital for Kotlin Coroutines to provide some
332â†’     * non-blocking guarantee, as users may add blocking code in [SelectBuilder], and this blocking code
333â†’     * should not cause blocking behaviour in other places, such as an attempt to make a rendezvous with
334â†’     * the `select` that is hang in REGISTRATION phase.
335â†’     *
336â†’     * Also, this implementation is NOT linearizable under some circumstances. The reason is that a rendezvous
337â†’     * attempt with `select` (via [SelectInstance.trySelect]) may fail when this `select` operation is still
338â†’     * in REGISTRATION phase. Consider the following situation on two empty rendezvous channels `c1` and `c2`
339â†’     * and the `select` operation that tries to send an element to one of these channels. First, this `select`
340â†’     * instance is registered as a waiter in `c1`. After that, another thread can observe that `c1` is no longer
341â†’     * empty and try to receive an element from `c1` -- this receive attempt fails due to the `select` operation
342â†’     * being in REGISTRATION phase.
343â†’     * It is also possible to observe that this `select` operation registered in `c2` first, and only after that in
344â†’     * `c1` (it has to re-register in `c1` after the unsuccessful rendezvous attempt), which is also non-linearizable.
345â†’     * We, however, find such a non-linearizable behaviour not so important in practice and leverage the correctness
346â†’     * relaxation for the algorithm simplicity and the non-blocking progress guarantee.
347â†’     */
348â†’
349â†’    /**
350â†’     * The state of this `select` operation. See the description above for details.
351â†’     */
352â†’    private val state = atomic<Any>(STATE_REG)
353â†’    /**
354â†’     * Returns `true` if this `select` instance is in the REGISTRATION phase;
355â†’     * otherwise, returns `false`.
356â†’     */
357â†’    private val inRegistrationPhase
358â†’        get() = state.value.let {
359â†’            it === STATE_REG || it is List<*>
360â†’        }
361â†’    /**
362â†’     * Returns `true` if this `select` is already selected;
363â†’     * thus, other parties are bound to fail when making a rendezvous with it.
364â†’     */
365â†’    private val isSelected
366â†’        get() = state.value is SelectImplementation<*>.ClauseData
367â†’    /**
368â†’     * Returns `true` if this `select` is cancelled.
369â†’     */
370â†’    private val isCancelled
371â†’        get() = state.value === STATE_CANCELLED
372â†’
373â†’    /**
374â†’     * List of clauses waiting on this `select` instance.
375â†’     *
376â†’     * This property is the subject to bening data race: concurrent cancellation might null-out this property
377â†’     * while [trySelect] operation reads it and iterates over its content.
378â†’     * A logical race is resolved by the consensus on [state] property.
379â†’     */
380â†’    @BenignDataRace
381â†’    private var clauses: MutableList<ClauseData>? = ArrayList(2)
382â†’
383â†’    /**
384â†’     * Stores the completion action provided through [disposeOnCompletion] or [invokeOnCancellation]
385â†’     * during clause registration. After that, if the clause is successfully registered
386â†’     * (so, it has not completed immediately), this handler is stored into
387â†’     * the corresponding [ClauseData] instance.
388â†’     *
389â†’     * Note that either [DisposableHandle] is provided, or a [Segment] instance with
390â†’     * the index in it, which specify the location of storing this `select`.
391â†’     * In the latter case, [Segment.onCancellation] should be called on completion/cancellation.
392â†’     */
393â†’    private var disposableHandleOrSegment: Any? = null
394â†’
395â†’    /**
396â†’     * In case the disposable handle is specified via [Segment]
397â†’     * and index in it, implying calling [Segment.onCancellation],
398â†’     * the corresponding index is stored in this field.
399â†’     * The segment is stored in [disposableHandleOrSegment].
400â†’     */
401â†’    private var indexInSegment: Int = -1
402â†’
403â†’    /**
404â†’     * Stores the result passed via [selectInRegistrationPhase] during clause registration
405â†’     * or [trySelect], which is called by another coroutine trying to make a rendezvous
406â†’     * with this `select` instance. Further, this result is processed via the
407â†’     * [ProcessResultFunction] of the selected clause.
408â†’     *
409â†’     * Unfortunately, we cannot store the result in the [state] field, as the latter stores
410â†’     * the clause object upon selection (see [ClauseData.clauseObject] and [SelectClause.clauseObject]).
411â†’     * Instead, it is possible to merge the [internalResult] and [disposableHandle] fields into
412â†’     * one that stores either result when the clause is successfully registered ([inRegistrationPhase] is `true`),
413â†’     * or [DisposableHandle] instance when the clause is completed during registration ([inRegistrationPhase] is `false`).
414â†’     * Yet, this optimization is omitted for code simplicity.
415â†’     *
416â†’     * This property is the subject to benign data race:
417â†’     * [Cleanup][cleanup] procedure can be invoked both as part of the completion sequence
418â†’     * and as a cancellation handler triggered by an external cancellation.
419â†’     * In both scenarios, [NO_RESULT] is written to this property via race.
420â†’     */
421â†’    @BenignDataRace
422â†’    private var internalResult: Any? = NO_RESULT
423â†’
424â†’    /**
425â†’     * This function is called after the [SelectBuilder] is applied. In case one of the clauses is already selected,
426â†’     * the algorithm applies the corresponding [ProcessResultFunction] and invokes the user-specified [block][ClauseData.block].
427â†’     * Otherwise, it moves this `select` to WAITING phase (re-registering clauses if needed), suspends until a rendezvous
428â†’     * is happened, and then completes the operation by applying the corresponding [ProcessResultFunction] and
429â†’     * invoking the user-specified [block][ClauseData.block].
430â†’     */
431â†’    @PublishedApi
432â†’    internal open suspend fun doSelect(): R =
433â†’        if (isSelected) complete()  // Fast path
434â†’        else doSelectSuspend()      // Slow path
435â†’
436â†’    // We separate the following logic as it has two suspension points
437â†’    // and, therefore, breaks the tail-call optimization if it were
438â†’    // inlined in [doSelect]
439â†’    private suspend fun doSelectSuspend(): R {
440â†’        // In case no clause has been selected during registration,
441â†’        // the `select` operation suspends and waits for a rendezvous.
442â†’        waitUntilSelected() // <-- suspend call => no tail-call optimization here
443â†’        // There is a selected clause! Apply the corresponding
444â†’        // [ProcessResultFunction] and invoke the user-specified block.
445â†’        return complete() // <-- one more suspend call
446â†’    }
447â†’
448â†’    // ========================
449â†’    // = CLAUSES REGISTRATION =
450â†’    // ========================
451â†’
452â†’    override fun SelectClause0.invoke(block: suspend () -> R) =
453â†’        ClauseData(clauseObject, regFunc, processResFunc, PARAM_CLAUSE_0, block, onCancellationConstructor).register()
454â†’    override fun <Q> SelectClause1<Q>.invoke(block: suspend (Q) -> R) =
455â†’        ClauseData(clauseObject, regFunc, processResFunc, null, block, onCancellationConstructor).register()
456â†’    override fun <P, Q> SelectClause2<P, Q>.invoke(param: P, block: suspend (Q) -> R) =
457â†’        ClauseData(clauseObject, regFunc, processResFunc, param, block, onCancellationConstructor).register()
458â†’
459â†’    /**
460â†’     * Attempts to register this `select` clause. If another clause is already selected,
461â†’     * this function does nothing and completes immediately.
462â†’     * Otherwise, it registers this `select` instance in
463â†’     * the [clause object][ClauseData.clauseObject]
464â†’     * according to the provided [registration function][ClauseData.regFunc].
465â†’     * On success, this `select` instance is stored as a waiter
466â†’     * in the clause object -- the algorithm also stores
467â†’     * the provided via [disposeOnCompletion] completion action
468â†’     * and adds the clause to the list of registered one.
469â†’     * In case of registration failure, the internal result
470â†’     * (not processed by [ProcessResultFunction] yet) must be
471â†’     * provided via [selectInRegistrationPhase] -- the algorithm
472â†’     * updates the state to this clause reference.
473â†’     */
474â†’    @JvmName("register")
475â†’    internal fun ClauseData.register(reregister: Boolean = false) {
476â†’        assert { state.value !== STATE_CANCELLED }
477â†’        // Is there already selected clause?
478â†’        if (state.value.let { it is SelectImplementation<*>.ClauseData }) return
479â†’        // For new clauses, check that there does not exist
480â†’        // another clause with the same object.
481â†’        if (!reregister) checkClauseObject(clauseObject)
482â†’        // Try to register in the corresponding object.
483â†’        if (tryRegisterAsWaiter(this@SelectImplementation)) {
484â†’            // Successfully registered, and this `select` instance
485â†’            // is stored as a waiter. Add this clause to the list
486â†’            // of registered clauses and store the provided via
487â†’            // [invokeOnCompletion] completion action into the clause.
488â†’            //
489â†’            // Importantly, the [waitUntilSelected] function is implemented
490â†’            // carefully to ensure that the cancellation handler has not been
491â†’            // installed when clauses re-register, so the logic below cannot
492â†’            // be invoked concurrently with the clean-up procedure.
493â†’            // This also guarantees that the list of clauses cannot be cleared
494â†’            // in the registration phase, so it is safe to read it with "!!".
495â†’            if (!reregister) clauses!! += this
496â†’            disposableHandleOrSegment = this@SelectImplementation.disposableHandleOrSegment
497â†’            indexInSegment = this@SelectImplementation.indexInSegment
498â†’            this@SelectImplementation.disposableHandleOrSegment = null
499â†’            this@SelectImplementation.indexInSegment = -1
500â†’        } else {
501â†’            // This clause has been selected!
502â†’            // Update the state correspondingly.
503â†’            state.value = this
504â†’        }
505â†’    }
506â†’
507â†’    /**
508â†’     * Checks that there does not exist another clause with the same object.
509â†’     */
510â†’    private fun checkClauseObject(clauseObject: Any) {
511â†’        // Read the list of clauses, it is guaranteed that it is non-null.
512â†’        // In fact, it can become `null` only in the clean-up phase, while
513â†’        // this check can be called only in the registration one.
514â†’        val clauses = clauses!!
515â†’        // Check that there does not exist another clause with the same object.
516â†’        check(clauses.none { it.clauseObject === clauseObject }) {
517â†’            "Cannot use select clauses on the same object: $clauseObject"
518â†’        }
519â†’    }
520â†’
521â†’    override fun disposeOnCompletion(disposableHandle: DisposableHandle) {
522â†’        this.disposableHandleOrSegment = disposableHandle
523â†’    }
524â†’
525â†’    /**
526â†’     * An optimized version for the code below that does not allocate
527â†’     * a cancellation handler object and efficiently stores the specified
528â†’     * [segment] and [index].
529â†’     *
530â†’     * ```
531â†’     * disposeOnCompletion {
532â†’     *   segment.onCancellation(index, null)
533â†’     * }
534â†’     * ```
535â†’     */
536â†’    override fun invokeOnCancellation(segment: Segment<*>, index: Int) {
537â†’        this.disposableHandleOrSegment = segment
538â†’        this.indexInSegment = index
539â†’    }
540â†’
541â†’    override fun selectInRegistrationPhase(internalResult: Any?) {
542â†’        this.internalResult = internalResult
543â†’    }
544â†’
545â†’    // =========================
546â†’    // = WAITING FOR SELECTION =
547â†’    // =========================
548â†’
549â†’    /**
550â†’     * Suspends and waits until some clause is selected. However, it is possible for a concurrent
551â†’     * coroutine to invoke [trySelect] while this `select` is still in REGISTRATION phase.
552â†’     * In this case, [trySelect] marks the corresponding select clause to be re-registered, and
553â†’     * this function performs registration of such clauses. After that, it atomically stores
554â†’     * the continuation into the [state] field if there is no more clause to be re-registered.
555â†’     */
556â†’    private suspend fun waitUntilSelected() = suspendCancellableCoroutine<Unit> sc@ { cont ->
557â†’        // Update the state.
558â†’        state.loop { curState ->
559â†’            when {
560â†’                // This `select` is in REGISTRATION phase, and there is no clause to be re-registered.
561â†’                // Perform a transition to WAITING phase by storing the current continuation.
562â†’                curState === STATE_REG -> if (state.compareAndSet(curState, cont)) {
563â†’                    // Perform a clean-up in case of cancellation.
564â†’                    //
565â†’                    // Importantly, we MUST install the cancellation handler
566â†’                    // only when the algorithm is bound to suspend. Otherwise,
567â†’                    // a race with [tryRegister] is possible, and the provided
568â†’                    // via [disposeOnCompletion] cancellation action can be ignored.
569â†’                    // Also, we MUST guarantee that this dispose handle is _visible_
570â†’                    // according to the memory model, and we CAN guarantee this when
571â†’                    // the state is updated.
572â†’                    cont.invokeOnCancellation(this.asHandler)
573â†’                    return@sc
574â†’                }
575â†’                // This `select` is in REGISTRATION phase, but there are clauses that has to be registered again.
576â†’                // Perform the required registrations and try again.
577â†’                curState is List<*> -> if (state.compareAndSet(curState, STATE_REG)) {
578â†’                    @Suppress("UNCHECKED_CAST")
579â†’                    curState as List<Any>
580â†’                    curState.forEach { reregisterClause(it) }
581â†’                }
582â†’                // This `select` operation became completed during clauses re-registration.
583â†’                curState is SelectImplementation<*>.ClauseData -> {
584â†’                    cont.resume(Unit, curState.createOnCancellationAction(this, internalResult))
585â†’                    return@sc
586â†’                }
587â†’                // This `select` cannot be in any other state.
588â†’                else -> error("unexpected state: $curState")
589â†’            }
590â†’        }
591â†’    }
592â†’
593â†’    /**
594â†’     * Re-registers the clause with the specified
595â†’     * [clause object][clauseObject] after unsuccessful
596â†’     * [trySelect] of this clause while the `select`
597â†’     * was still in REGISTRATION phase.
598â†’     */
599â†’    private fun reregisterClause(clauseObject: Any) {
600â†’        val clause = findClause(clauseObject)!! // it is guaranteed that the corresponding clause is presented
601â†’        clause.disposableHandleOrSegment = null
602â†’        clause.indexInSegment = -1

41â†’    @Suppress("UNCHECKED_CAST")

48â†’    @Suppress("UNUSED_PARAMETER")
49â†’    private fun register(select: SelectInstance<*>, ignoredParam: Any?) {

119â†’ * Runs a given suspending block of code inside a coroutine with the specified [timeout] and returns
120â†’ * `null` if this timeout was exceeded.
121â†’ * If the given [timeout] is non-positive, `null` is returned immediately.
122â†’ *
123â†’ * The code that is executing inside the [block] is cancelled on timeout and the active or next invocation of
124â†’ * cancellable suspending function inside the block throws a [TimeoutCancellationException].
125â†’ *
126â†’ * The sibling function that throws an exception on timeout is [withTimeout].
127â†’ * Note that the timeout action can be specified for a [select] invocation with [onTimeout][SelectBuilder.onTimeout] clause.
128â†’ *
129â†’ * **The timeout event is asynchronous with respect to the code running in the block** and may happen at any time,
130â†’ * even right before the return from inside the timeout [block]. Keep this in mind if you open or acquire some
131â†’ * resource inside the [block] that needs closing or release outside the block.
132â†’ * See the
133â†’ * [Asynchronous timeout and resources][https://kotlinlang.org/docs/reference/coroutines/cancellation-and-timeouts.html#asynchronous-timeout-and-resources]
134â†’ * section of the coroutines guide for details.
135â†’ *
136â†’ * > Implementation note: how the time is tracked exactly is an implementation detail of the context's [CoroutineDispatcher].
137â†’ */
138â†’public suspend fun <T> withTimeoutOrNull(timeout: Duration, block: suspend CoroutineScope.() -> T): T? =
139â†’    withTimeoutOrNull(timeout.toDelayMillis(), block)
140â†’
141â†’private fun <U, T : U> setupTimeout(
142â†’    coroutine: TimeoutCoroutine<U, T>,
143â†’    block: suspend CoroutineScope.() -> T
144â†’): Any? {
145â†’    // schedule cancellation of this coroutine on time
146â†’    val cont = coroutine.uCont
147â†’    val context = cont.context
148â†’    coroutine.disposeOnCompletion(context.delay.invokeOnTimeout(coroutine.time, coroutine, coroutine.context))
149â†’    // restart the block using a new coroutine with a new job,
150â†’    // however, start it undispatched, because we already are in the proper context
151â†’    return coroutine.startUndispatchedOrReturnIgnoreTimeout(coroutine, block)
152â†’}
153â†’
154â†’private class TimeoutCoroutine<U, in T : U>(
155â†’    @JvmField val time: Long,
156â†’    uCont: Continuation<U> // unintercepted continuation
157â†’) : ScopeCoroutine<T>(uCont.context, uCont), Runnable {
158â†’    override fun run() {
159â†’        cancelCoroutine(TimeoutCancellationException(time, context.delay, this))
160â†’    }
161â†’
162â†’    override fun nameString(): String =
163â†’        "${super.nameString()}(timeMillis=$time)"
164â†’}
165â†’
166â†’/**
167â†’ * This exception is thrown by [withTimeout] to indicate timeout.
168â†’ */
169â†’public class TimeoutCancellationException internal constructor(
170â†’    message: String,
171â†’    @JvmField @Transient internal val coroutine: Job?
172â†’) : CancellationException(message), CopyableThrowable<TimeoutCancellationException> {
173â†’    /**
174â†’     * Creates a timeout exception with the given message.
175â†’     * This constructor is needed for exception stack-traces recovery.
176â†’     */
177â†’    internal constructor(message: String) : this(message, null)
178â†’
179â†’    // message is never null in fact
180â†’    override fun createCopy(): TimeoutCancellationException =
181â†’        TimeoutCancellationException(message ?: "", coroutine).also { it.initCause(this) }
182â†’}
183â†’
184â†’internal fun TimeoutCancellationException(
185â†’    time: Long,
186â†’    delay: Delay,
187â†’    coroutine: Job
188â†’) : TimeoutCancellationException {
189â†’    val message = (delay as? DelayWithTimeoutDiagnostics)?.timeoutMessage(time.milliseconds)
190â†’        ?: "Timed out waiting for $time ms"
191â†’    return TimeoutCancellationException(message, coroutine)
192â†’}

  5â†’package kotlinx.coroutines.flow
  6â†’
  7â†’import kotlinx.coroutines.*
  8â†’import kotlinx.coroutines.internal.IgnoreJreRequirement

 10â†’
 11â†’/**
 12â†’ * A command emitted by [SharingStarted] implementations to control the sharing coroutine in
 13â†’ * the [shareIn] and [stateIn] operators.
 14â†’ */
 15â†’public enum class SharingCommand {
 16â†’    /**
 17â†’     * Starts sharing, launching collection of the upstream flow.
 18â†’     *
 19â†’     * Emitting this command again does not do anything. Emit [STOP] and then [START] to restart an
 20â†’     * upstream flow.
 21â†’     */
 22â†’    START,
 23â†’
 24â†’    /**
 25â†’     * Stops sharing, cancelling collection of the upstream flow.
 26â†’     */
 27â†’    STOP,
 28â†’
 29â†’    /**
 30â†’     * Stops sharing, cancelling collection of the upstream flow, and resets the [SharedFlow.replayCache]
 31â†’     * to its initial state.
 32â†’     * The [shareIn] operator calls [MutableSharedFlow.resetReplayCache];
 33â†’     * the [stateIn] operator resets the value to its original `initialValue`.
 34â†’     */
 35â†’    STOP_AND_RESET_REPLAY_CACHE
 36â†’}
 37â†’
 38â†’/**
 39â†’ * A strategy for starting and stopping the sharing coroutine in [shareIn] and [stateIn] operators.
 40â†’ *
 41â†’ * This functional interface provides a set of built-in strategies: [Eagerly], [Lazily], [WhileSubscribed], and
 42â†’ * supports custom strategies by implementing this interface's [command] function.
 43â†’ *
 44â†’ * For example, it is possible to define a custom strategy that starts the upstream only when the number
 45â†’ * of subscribers exceeds the given `threshold` and make it an extension on [SharingStarted.Companion] so
 46â†’ * that it looks like a built-in strategy on the use-site:
 47â†’ *
 48â†’ * ```
 49â†’ * fun SharingStarted.Companion.WhileSubscribedAtLeast(threshold: Int) =
 50â†’ *     SharingStarted { subscriptionCount: StateFlow<Int> ->
 51â†’ *         subscriptionCount.map { if (it >= threshold) SharingCommand.START else SharingCommand.STOP }
 52â†’ *     }
 53â†’ * ```
 54â†’ *
 55â†’ * ### Commands
 56â†’ *
 57â†’ * The `SharingStarted` strategy works by emitting [commands][SharingCommand] that control upstream flow from its
 58â†’ * [`command`][command] flow implementation function. Back-to-back emissions of the same command have no effect.
 59â†’ * Only emission of a different command has effect:
 60â†’ *
 61â†’ * * [START][SharingCommand.START] — the upstream flow is started.
 62â†’ * * [STOP][SharingCommand.STOP] — the upstream flow is stopped.
 63â†’ * * [STOP_AND_RESET_REPLAY_CACHE][SharingCommand.STOP_AND_RESET_REPLAY_CACHE] —
 64â†’ *   the upstream flow is stopped and the [SharedFlow.replayCache] is reset to its initial state.
 65â†’ *   The [shareIn] operator calls [MutableSharedFlow.resetReplayCache];
 66â†’ *   the [stateIn] operator resets the value to its original `initialValue`.
 67â†’ *   
 68â†’ * Initially, the upstream flow is stopped and is in the initial state, so the emission of additional
 69â†’ * [STOP][SharingCommand.STOP] and [STOP_AND_RESET_REPLAY_CACHE][SharingCommand.STOP_AND_RESET_REPLAY_CACHE] commands will
 70â†’ * have no effect.
 71â†’ *
 72â†’ * The completion of the `command` flow normally has no effect (the upstream flow keeps running if it was running).
 73â†’ * The failure of the `command` flow cancels the sharing coroutine and the upstream flow.
 74â†’ */
 75â†’public fun interface SharingStarted {
 76â†’    public companion object {
 77â†’        /**
 78â†’         * Sharing is started immediately and never stops.
 79â†’         */
 80â†’        public val Eagerly: SharingStarted = StartedEagerly()
 81â†’
 82â†’        /**
 83â†’         * Sharing is started when the first subscriber appears and never stops.
 84â†’         */
 85â†’        public val Lazily: SharingStarted = StartedLazily()
 86â†’
 87â†’        /**
 88â†’         * Sharing is started when the first subscriber appears, immediately stops when the last
 89â†’         * subscriber disappears (by default), keeping the replay cache forever (by default).
 90â†’         *
 91â†’         * It has the following optional parameters:
 92â†’         *
 93â†’         * * [stopTimeoutMillis] — configures a delay (in milliseconds) between the disappearance of the last
 94â†’         *   subscriber and the stopping of the sharing coroutine. It defaults to zero (stop immediately).
 95â†’         * * [replayExpirationMillis] — configures a delay (in milliseconds) between the stopping of
 96â†’         *   the sharing coroutine and the resetting of the replay cache (which makes the cache empty for the [shareIn] operator
 97â†’         *   and resets the cached value to the original `initialValue` for the [stateIn] operator).
 98â†’         *   It defaults to `Long.MAX_VALUE` (keep replay cache forever, never reset buffer).
 99â†’         *   Use zero value to expire the cache immediately.
100â†’         *
101â†’         * This function throws [IllegalArgumentException] when either [stopTimeoutMillis] or [replayExpirationMillis]
102â†’         * are negative.
103â†’         */
104â†’        @Suppress("FunctionName")
105â†’        public fun WhileSubscribed(
106â†’            stopTimeoutMillis: Long = 0,
107â†’            replayExpirationMillis: Long = Long.MAX_VALUE
108â†’        ): SharingStarted =
109â†’            StartedWhileSubscribed(stopTimeoutMillis, replayExpirationMillis)
110â†’    }
111â†’
112â†’    /**
113â†’     * Transforms the [subscriptionCount][MutableSharedFlow.subscriptionCount] state of the shared flow into the
114â†’     * flow of [commands][SharingCommand] that control the sharing coroutine. See the [SharingStarted] interface
115â†’     * documentation for details.
116â†’     */
117â†’    public fun command(subscriptionCount: StateFlow<Int>): Flow<SharingCommand>
118â†’}
119â†’
120â†’/**
121â†’ * Sharing is started when the first subscriber appears, immediately stops when the last
122â†’ * subscriber disappears (by default), keeping the replay cache forever (by default).
123â†’ *
124â†’ * It has the following optional parameters:
125â†’ *
126â†’ * * [stopTimeout] — configures a delay between the disappearance of the last
127â†’ *   subscriber and the stopping of the sharing coroutine. It defaults to zero (stop immediately).
128â†’ * * [replayExpiration] — configures a delay between the stopping of
129â†’ *   the sharing coroutine and the resetting of the replay cache (which makes the cache empty for the [shareIn] operator
130â†’ *   and resets the cached value to the original `initialValue` for the [stateIn] operator).
131â†’ *   It defaults to [Duration.INFINITE] (keep replay cache forever, never reset buffer).
132â†’ *   Use [Duration.ZERO] value to expire the cache immediately.
133â†’ *
134â†’ * This function throws [IllegalArgumentException] when either [stopTimeout] or [replayExpiration]
135â†’ * are negative.
136â†’ */
137â†’@Suppress("FunctionName")
138â†’public fun SharingStarted.Companion.WhileSubscribed(
139â†’    stopTimeout: Duration = Duration.ZERO,
140â†’    replayExpiration: Duration = Duration.INFINITE
141â†’): SharingStarted =
142â†’    StartedWhileSubscribed(stopTimeout.inWholeMilliseconds, replayExpiration.inWholeMilliseconds)
143â†’
144â†’// -------------------------------- implementation --------------------------------
145â†’
146â†’private class StartedEagerly : SharingStarted {
147â†’    override fun command(subscriptionCount: StateFlow<Int>): Flow<SharingCommand> =
148â†’        flowOf(SharingCommand.START)
149â†’    override fun toString(): String = "SharingStarted.Eagerly"
150â†’}
151â†’
152â†’private class StartedLazily : SharingStarted {
153â†’    override fun command(subscriptionCount: StateFlow<Int>): Flow<SharingCommand> = flow {
154â†’        var started = false
155â†’        subscriptionCount.collect { count ->
156â†’            if (count > 0 && !started) {
157â†’                started = true
158â†’                emit(SharingCommand.START)
159â†’            }
160â†’        }
161â†’    }
162â†’
163â†’    override fun toString(): String = "SharingStarted.Lazily"
164â†’}
165â†’
166â†’private class StartedWhileSubscribed(
167â†’    private val stopTimeout: Long,
168â†’    private val replayExpiration: Long
169â†’) : SharingStarted {
170â†’    init {
171â†’        require(stopTimeout >= 0) { "stopTimeout($stopTimeout ms) cannot be negative" }
172â†’        require(replayExpiration >= 0) { "replayExpiration($replayExpiration ms) cannot be negative" }
173â†’    }
174â†’
175â†’    override fun command(subscriptionCount: StateFlow<Int>): Flow<SharingCommand> = subscriptionCount
176â†’        .transformLatest { count ->
177â†’            if (count > 0) {
178â†’                emit(SharingCommand.START)
179â†’            } else {
180â†’                delay(stopTimeout)
181â†’                if (replayExpiration > 0) {
182â†’                    emit(SharingCommand.STOP)
183â†’                    delay(replayExpiration)
184â†’                }
185â†’                emit(SharingCommand.STOP_AND_RESET_REPLAY_CACHE)
186â†’            }
187â†’        }
188â†’        .dropWhile { it != SharingCommand.START } // don't emit any STOP/RESET_BUFFER to start with, only START
189â†’        .distinctUntilChanged() // just in case somebody forgets it, don't leak our multiple sending of START
190â†’
191â†’    @OptIn(ExperimentalStdlibApi::class)
192â†’    override fun toString(): String {
193â†’        val params = buildList(2) {
194â†’            if (stopTimeout > 0) add("stopTimeout=${stopTimeout}ms")
195â†’            if (replayExpiration < Long.MAX_VALUE) add("replayExpiration=${replayExpiration}ms")
196â†’        }
197â†’        return "SharingStarted.WhileSubscribed(${params.joinToString()})"
198â†’    }
199â†’
200â†’    // equals & hashcode to facilitate testing, not documented in public contract
201â†’    override fun equals(other: Any?): Boolean =
202â†’        other is StartedWhileSubscribed &&
203â†’            stopTimeout == other.stopTimeout &&
204â†’            replayExpiration == other.replayExpiration
205â†’
206â†’    @IgnoreJreRequirement // desugared hashcode implementation
207â†’    override fun hashCode(): Int = stopTimeout.hashCode() * 31 + replayExpiration.hashCode()
208â†’}

  7â†’import kotlinx.atomicfu.*
  8â†’import kotlinx.coroutines.*
  9â†’import kotlinx.coroutines.channels.*
 10â†’import kotlinx.coroutines.channels.Channel.Factory.CONFLATED
 11â†’import kotlinx.coroutines.internal.*
 12â†’import kotlinx.coroutines.selects.*
 13â†’import kotlin.coroutines.*
 14â†’import kotlin.jvm.*
 15â†’import kotlin.time.*
 16â†’import kotlin.time.Duration.Companion.milliseconds

 18â†’/**
 19â†’ * This is a scheduler for coroutines used in tests, providing the delay-skipping behavior.
 20â†’ *
 21â†’ * [Test dispatchers][TestDispatcher] are parameterized with a scheduler. Several dispatchers can share the
 22â†’ * same scheduler, in which case their knowledge about the virtual time will be synchronized. When the dispatchers
 23â†’ * require scheduling an event at a later point in time, they notify the scheduler, which will establish the order of
 24â†’ * the tasks.
 25â†’ *
 26â†’ * The scheduler can be queried to advance the time (via [advanceTimeBy]), run all the scheduled tasks advancing the
 27â†’ * virtual time as needed (via [advanceUntilIdle]), or run the tasks that are scheduled to run as soon as possible but
 28â†’ * haven't yet been dispatched (via [runCurrent]).
 29â†’ */
 30â†’public class TestCoroutineScheduler : AbstractCoroutineContextElement(TestCoroutineScheduler),
 31â†’    CoroutineContext.Element {
 32â†’
 33â†’    /** @suppress */
 34â†’    public companion object Key : CoroutineContext.Key<TestCoroutineScheduler>
 35â†’
 36â†’    /** This heap stores the knowledge about which dispatchers are interested in which moments of virtual time. */
 37â†’    // TODO: all the synchronization is done via a separate lock, so a non-thread-safe priority queue can be used.
 38â†’    private val events = ThreadSafeHeap<TestDispatchEvent<Any>>()
 39â†’
 40â†’    /** Establishes that [currentTime] can't exceed the time of the earliest event in [events]. */
 41â†’    private val lock = SynchronizedObject()
 42â†’
 43â†’    /** This counter establishes some order on the events that happen at the same virtual time. */
 44â†’    private val count = atomic(0L)
 45â†’
 46â†’    /** The current virtual time in milliseconds. */
 47â†’    @ExperimentalCoroutinesApi
 48â†’    public var currentTime: Long = 0
 49â†’        get() = synchronized(lock) { field }
 50â†’        private set
 51â†’
 52â†’    /** A channel for notifying about the fact that a foreground work dispatch recently happened. */
 53â†’    private val dispatchEventsForeground: Channel<Unit> = Channel(CONFLATED)
 54â†’
 55â†’    /** A channel for notifying about the fact that a dispatch recently happened. */
 56â†’    private val dispatchEvents: Channel<Unit> = Channel(CONFLATED)
 57â†’
 58â†’    /**
 59â†’     * Registers a request for the scheduler to notify [dispatcher] at a virtual moment [timeDeltaMillis] milliseconds
 60â†’     * later via [TestDispatcher.processEvent], which will be called with the provided [marker] object.
 61â†’     *
 62â†’     * Returns the handler which can be used to cancel the registration.
 63â†’     */
 64â†’    internal fun <T : Any> registerEvent(
 65â†’        dispatcher: TestDispatcher,
 66â†’        timeDeltaMillis: Long,
 67â†’        marker: T,
 68â†’        context: CoroutineContext,
 69â†’        isCancelled: (T) -> Boolean
 70â†’    ): DisposableHandle {
 71â†’        require(timeDeltaMillis >= 0) { "Attempted scheduling an event earlier in time (with the time delta $timeDeltaMillis)" }
 72â†’        checkSchedulerInContext(this, context)
 73â†’        val count = count.getAndIncrement()
 74â†’        val isForeground = context[BackgroundWork] === null
 75â†’        return synchronized(lock) {
 76â†’            val time = addClamping(currentTime, timeDeltaMillis)
 77â†’            val event = TestDispatchEvent(dispatcher, count, time, marker as Any, isForeground) { isCancelled(marker) }
 78â†’            events.addLast(event)
 79â†’            /** can't be moved above: otherwise, [onDispatchEventForeground] or [onDispatchEvent] could consume the
 80â†’             * token sent here before there's actually anything in the event queue. */
 81â†’            sendDispatchEvent(context)
 82â†’            DisposableHandle {
 83â†’                synchronized(lock) {
 84â†’                    events.remove(event)
 85â†’                }
 86â†’            }
 87â†’        }
 88â†’    }
 89â†’
 90â†’    /**
 91â†’     * Runs the next enqueued task, advancing the virtual time to the time of its scheduled awakening,
 92â†’     * unless [condition] holds.
 93â†’     */
 94â†’    internal fun tryRunNextTaskUnless(condition: () -> Boolean): Boolean {
 95â†’        val event = synchronized(lock) {
 96â†’            if (condition()) return false
 97â†’            val event = events.removeFirstOrNull() ?: return false
 98â†’            if (currentTime > event.time)
 99â†’                currentTimeAheadOfEvents()
100â†’            currentTime = event.time
101â†’            event
102â†’        }
103â†’        event.dispatcher.processEvent(event.marker)
104â†’        return true
105â†’    }
106â†’
107â†’    /**
108â†’     * Runs the enqueued tasks in the specified order, advancing the virtual time as needed until there are no more
109â†’     * tasks associated with the dispatchers linked to this scheduler.
110â†’     *
111â†’     * A breaking change from `TestCoroutineDispatcher.advanceTimeBy` is that it no longer returns the total number of
112â†’     * milliseconds by which the execution of this method has advanced the virtual time. If you want to recreate that
113â†’     * functionality, query [currentTime] before and after the execution to achieve the same result.
114â†’     */
115â†’    public fun advanceUntilIdle(): Unit = advanceUntilIdleOr { events.none(TestDispatchEvent<*>::isForeground) }
116â†’
117â†’    /**
118â†’     * [condition]: guaranteed to be invoked under the lock.
119â†’     */
120â†’    internal fun advanceUntilIdleOr(condition: () -> Boolean) {
121â†’        while (true) {
122â†’            if (!tryRunNextTaskUnless(condition))
123â†’                return
124â†’        }
125â†’    }
126â†’
127â†’    /**
128â†’     * Runs the tasks that are scheduled to execute at this moment of virtual time.
129â†’     */
130â†’    public fun runCurrent() {
131â†’        val timeMark = synchronized(lock) { currentTime }
132â†’        while (true) {
133â†’            val event = synchronized(lock) {
134â†’                events.removeFirstIf { it.time <= timeMark } ?: return
135â†’            }
136â†’            event.dispatcher.processEvent(event.marker)
137â†’        }
138â†’    }
139â†’
140â†’    /**
141â†’     * Moves the virtual clock of this dispatcher forward by [the specified amount][delayTimeMillis], running the
142â†’     * scheduled tasks in the meantime.
143â†’     *
144â†’     * Breaking changes from [TestCoroutineDispatcher.advanceTimeBy]:
145â†’     * * Intentionally doesn't return a `Long` value, as its use cases are unclear. We may restore it in the future;
146â†’     *   please describe your use cases at [the issue tracker](https://github.com/Kotlin/kotlinx.coroutines/issues/).
147â†’     *   For now, it's possible to query [currentTime] before and after execution of this method, to the same effect.
148â†’     * * It doesn't run the tasks that are scheduled at exactly [currentTime] + [delayTimeMillis]. For example,
149â†’     *   advancing the time by one millisecond used to run the tasks at the current millisecond *and* the next
150â†’     *   millisecond, but now will stop just before executing any task starting at the next millisecond.
151â†’     * * Overflowing the target time used to lead to nothing being done, but will now run the tasks scheduled at up to
152â†’     *   (but not including) [Long.MAX_VALUE].
153â†’     *
154â†’     * @throws IllegalArgumentException if passed a negative [delay][delayTimeMillis].
155â†’     */
156â†’    @ExperimentalCoroutinesApi
157â†’    public fun advanceTimeBy(delayTimeMillis: Long): Unit = advanceTimeBy(delayTimeMillis.milliseconds)
158â†’
159â†’    /**
160â†’     * Moves the virtual clock of this dispatcher forward by [the specified amount][delayTime], running the
161â†’     * scheduled tasks in the meantime.
162â†’     *
163â†’     * @throws IllegalArgumentException if passed a negative [delay][delayTime].
164â†’     */
165â†’    public fun advanceTimeBy(delayTime: Duration) {
166â†’        require(!delayTime.isNegative()) { "Can not advance time by a negative delay: $delayTime" }
167â†’        val startingTime = currentTime
168â†’        val targetTime = addClamping(startingTime, delayTime.inWholeMilliseconds)
169â†’        while (true) {
170â†’            val event = synchronized(lock) {
171â†’                val timeMark = currentTime
172â†’                val event = events.removeFirstIf { targetTime > it.time }
173â†’                when {
174â†’                    event == null -> {
175â†’                        currentTime = targetTime
176â†’                        return
177â†’                    }
178â†’                    timeMark > event.time -> currentTimeAheadOfEvents()
179â†’                    else -> {
180â†’                        currentTime = event.time
181â†’                        event
182â†’                    }

185â†’            event.dispatcher.processEvent(event.marker)

189â†’    /**
190â†’     * Checks that the only tasks remaining in the scheduler are cancelled.
191â†’     */
192â†’    internal fun isIdle(strict: Boolean = true): Boolean =
193â†’        synchronized(lock) {
194â†’            if (strict) events.isEmpty else events.none { !it.isCancelled() }
195â†’        }
196â†’
197â†’    /**
198â†’     * Notifies this scheduler about a dispatch event.
199â†’     *
200â†’     * [context] is the context in which the task will be dispatched.

202â†’    internal fun sendDispatchEvent(context: CoroutineContext) {

208â†’    /**

210â†’     */

212â†’
213â†’    /**
214â†’     * Consumes the knowledge that a dispatch event happened recently.
215â†’     */
216â†’    internal val onDispatchEvent: SelectClause1<Unit> get() = dispatchEvents.onReceive

221â†’    internal val onDispatchEventForeground: SelectClause1<Unit> get() = dispatchEventsForeground.onReceive

225â†’     */
226â†’    @ExperimentalTime
227â†’    public val timeSource: TimeSource.WithComparableMarks = object : AbstractLongTimeSource(DurationUnit.MILLISECONDS) {
228â†’        override fun read(): Long = currentTime

234â†’
235â†’private fun invalidSchedulerState(): Nothing =
236â†’    throw IllegalStateException("The test scheduler entered an invalid state. Please report this at https://github.com/Kotlin/kotlinx.coroutines/issues.")

238â†’/** [ThreadSafeHeap] node representing a scheduled task, ordered by the planned execution time. */
239â†’private class TestDispatchEvent<T>(
240â†’    @JvmField val dispatcher: TestDispatcher,
241â†’    private val count: Long,
242â†’    @JvmField val time: Long,
243â†’    @JvmField val marker: T,
244â†’    @JvmField val isForeground: Boolean,

247â†’) : Comparable<TestDispatchEvent<*>>, ThreadSafeHeapNode {

249â†’    override var index: Int = 0
250â†’
251â†’    override fun compareTo(other: TestDispatchEvent<*>) =
252â†’        compareValuesBy(this, other, TestDispatchEvent<*>::time, TestDispatchEvent<*>::count)
253â†’
254â†’    override fun toString() = "TestDispatchEvent(time=$time, dispatcher=$dispatcher${if (isForeground) "" else ", background"})"
255â†’}
256â†’
257â†’// works with positive `a`, `b`
258â†’private fun addClamping(a: Long, b: Long): Long = (a + b).let { if (it >= 0) it else Long.MAX_VALUE }

260â†’internal fun checkSchedulerInContext(scheduler: TestCoroutineScheduler, context: CoroutineContext) {
261â†’    context[TestCoroutineScheduler]?.let {

281â†’    find(predicate) == null

 65â†’    private fun testStress(replay: Int, started: SharingStarted) = runTest {
 66â†’        log("-- Stress with replay=$replay, started=$started")
 67â†’        val random = Random(1)
 68â†’        val emitIndex = AtomicLong()
 69â†’        val cancelledEmits = HashSet<Long>()
 70â†’        val missingCollects = Collections.synchronizedSet(LinkedHashSet<Long>())
 71â†’        // at most one copy of upstream can be running at any time
 72â†’        val isRunning = AtomicInteger(0)
 73â†’        val upstream = flow {
 74â†’            assertEquals(0, isRunning.getAndIncrement())
 75â†’            try {
 76â†’                while (true) {
 77â†’                    val value = emitIndex.getAndIncrement()
 78â†’                    try {
 79â†’                        emit(value)
 80â†’                    } catch (e: CancellationException) {
 81â†’                        // emission was cancelled -> could be missing
 82â†’                        cancelledEmits.add(value)
 83â†’                        throw e
 84â†’                    }
 85â†’                }
 86â†’            } finally {
 87â†’                assertEquals(1, isRunning.getAndDecrement())
 88â†’            }
 89â†’        }
 90â†’        val subCount = MutableStateFlow(0)
 91â†’        val sharingJob = Job()
 92â†’        val sharingScope = this + emitterDispatcher + sharingJob
 93â†’        val usingStateFlow = replay == 1
 94â†’        val sharedFlow = if (usingStateFlow)
 95â†’            upstream.stateIn(sharingScope, started, 0L)
 96â†’        else
 97â†’            upstream.shareIn(sharingScope, started, replay)
 98â†’        try {
 99â†’            val subscribers = ArrayList<SubJob>()
100â†’            withTimeoutOrNull(testDuration) {
101â†’                // start and stop subscribers
102â†’                while (true) {
103â†’                    log("Staring $nSubscribers subscribers")
104â†’                    repeat(nSubscribers) {
105â†’                        subscribers += launchSubscriber(sharedFlow, usingStateFlow, subCount, missingCollects)
106â†’                    }
107â†’                    // wait until they all subscribed
108â†’                    subCount.first { it == nSubscribers }
109â†’                    // let them work a bit more & make sure emitter did not hang
110â†’                    val fromEmitIndex = emitIndex.get()
111â†’                    val waitEmitIndex = fromEmitIndex + 100 // wait until 100 emitted
112â†’                    withTimeout(10000) { // wait for at most 10s for something to be emitted
113â†’                        do {
114â†’                            delay(random.nextLong(50L..100L))
115â†’                        } while (emitIndex.get() < waitEmitIndex)  // Ok, enough was emitted, wait more if not
116â†’                    }
117â†’                    // Stop all subscribers and ensure they collected something
118â†’                    log("Stopping subscribers (emitted = ${emitIndex.get() - fromEmitIndex})")
119â†’                    subscribers.forEach {
120â†’                        it.job.cancelAndJoin()
121â†’                        assertTrue { it.count > 0 } // something must be collected too
122â†’                    }
123â†’                    subscribers.clear()
124â†’                    log("Intermission")
125â†’                    delay(random.nextLong(10L..100L)) // wait a bit before starting them again
126â†’                }
127â†’            }
128â†’            if (!subscribers.isEmpty()) {
129â†’                log("Stopping subscribers")
130â†’                subscribers.forEach { it.job.cancelAndJoin() }
131â†’            }
132â†’        } finally {
133â†’            log("--- Finally: Cancelling sharing job")
134â†’            sharingJob.cancel()
135â†’        }
136â†’        sharingJob.join() // make sure sharing job did not hang
137â†’        log("Emitter was cancelled ${cancelledEmits.size} times")
138â†’        log("Collectors missed ${missingCollects.size} values")
139â†’        for (value in missingCollects) {
140â†’            assertTrue(value in cancelledEmits, "Value $value is missing for no apparent reason")
141â†’        }
142â†’    }
143â†’
144â†’    private fun CoroutineScope.launchSubscriber(
145â†’        sharedFlow: SharedFlow<Long>,
146â†’        usingStateFlow: Boolean,
147â†’        subCount: MutableStateFlow<Int>,
148â†’        missingCollects: MutableSet<Long>
149â†’    ): SubJob {
150â†’        val subJob = SubJob()
151â†’        subJob.job = launch(subscriberDispatcher) {
152â†’            var last = -1L
153â†’            sharedFlow
154â†’                .onSubscription {
155â†’                    subCount.increment(1)
156â†’                }
157â†’                .onCompletion {
158â†’                    subCount.increment(-1)
159â†’                }

161â†’                    subJob.count++
162â†’                    // last must grow sequentially, no jumping or losses
163â†’                    if (last == -1L) {
164â†’                        last = j
165â†’                    } else {
166â†’                        val expected = last + 1
167â†’                        if (usingStateFlow)
168â†’                            assertTrue(expected <= j)
169â†’                        else {
170â†’                            if (expected != j) {
171â†’                                if (j == expected + 1) {
172â†’                                    // if missing just one -- could be race with cancelled emit
173â†’                                    missingCollects.add(expected)
174â†’                                } else {
175â†’                                    // broken otherwise
176â†’                                    assertEquals(expected, j)
177â†’                                }
178â†’                            }
179â†’                        }
180â†’                        last = j
181â†’                    }
182â†’                }
183â†’        }
184â†’        return subJob
185â†’    }

189â†’        var count = 0L

54â†’/**
55â†’ * Coerces the given [Duration] to a millisecond delay.
56â†’ * Negative values are coerced to zero, values that cannot
57â†’ * be represented in milliseconds as long ("infinite" duration) are coerced to [Long.MAX_VALUE]
58â†’ * and durations lesser than a millisecond are coerced to 1 millisecond.
59â†’ *
60â†’ * The rationale of coercion:
61â†’ * 1) Too large durations typically indicate infinity and Long.MAX_VALUE is the
62â†’ *    best approximation of infinity we can provide.
63â†’ * 2) Coercing too small durations to 1 instead of 0 is crucial for two patterns:
64â†’ *    - Programming with deadlines and delays
65â†’ *    - Non-suspending fast-paths (e.g. `withTimeout(1 nanosecond) { 42 }` should not throw)
66â†’ */
67â†’private fun Duration.coerceToMillis(): Long {
68â†’    if (this <= Duration.ZERO) return 0
69â†’    if (this <= ChronoUnit.MILLIS.duration) return 1
70â†’
71â†’    // Maximum scalar values of Duration.ofMillis(Long.MAX_VALUE)
72â†’    val maxSeconds = 9223372036854775
73â†’    val maxNanos = 807000000
74â†’    return if (seconds < maxSeconds || seconds == maxSeconds && nano < maxNanos) toMillis()
75â†’    else Long.MAX_VALUE
76â†’}

 19â†’     * **This is a default mode.**
 20â†’     *
 21â†’     * ```
 22â†’     * val channel = ticker(delay = 100)
 23â†’     * delay(350) // 250 ms late
 24â†’     * println(channel.tryReceive().getOrNull()) // prints Unit
 25â†’     * println(channel.tryReceive().getOrNull()) // prints null
 26â†’     *
 27â†’     * delay(50)
 28â†’     * println(channel.tryReceive().getOrNull()) // prints Unit, delay was adjusted
 29â†’     * delay(50)
 30â†’     * println(channel.tryReceive().getOrNull()) // prints null, we're not late relatively to previous element
 31â†’     * ```
 32â†’     */
 33â†’    FIXED_PERIOD,
 34â†’
 35â†’    /**
 36â†’     * Maintains fixed delay between produced elements if consumer cannot keep up or it otherwise slow.
 37â†’     */
 38â†’    FIXED_DELAY

 41â†’/**
 42â†’ * Creates a channel that produces the first item after the given initial delay and subsequent items with the
 43â†’ * given delay between them.
 44â†’ *
 45â†’ * The resulting channel is a _rendezvous channel_. When receiver from this channel does not keep
 46â†’ * up with receiving the elements from this channel, they are not being sent due to backpressure. The actual
 47â†’ * timing behavior of ticker in this case is controlled by [mode] parameter which
 48â†’ * is set to [TickerMode.FIXED_PERIOD] by default. See [TickerMode] for other details.
 49â†’ *
 50â†’ * This channel stops producing elements immediately after [ReceiveChannel.cancel] invocation.
 51â†’ *
 52â†’ * **Note** producer to this channel is dispatched via [Dispatchers.Unconfined] by default and started eagerly.
 53â†’ *
 54â†’ * **Note: Ticker channels are not currently integrated with structured concurrency and their api will change in the future.**
 55â†’ *           
 56â†’ * @param delayMillis delay between each element in milliseconds.
 57â†’ * @param initialDelayMillis delay after which the first element will be produced (it is equal to [delayMillis] by default) in milliseconds.
 58â†’ * @param context context of the producing coroutine.
 59â†’ * @param mode specifies behavior when elements are not received ([FIXED_PERIOD][TickerMode.FIXED_PERIOD] by default).
 60â†’ */
 61â†’@ObsoleteCoroutinesApi
 62â†’public fun ticker(
 63â†’    delayMillis: Long,
 64â†’    initialDelayMillis: Long = delayMillis,
 65â†’    context: CoroutineContext = EmptyCoroutineContext,
 66â†’    mode: TickerMode = TickerMode.FIXED_PERIOD
 67â†’): ReceiveChannel<Unit> {
 68â†’    require(delayMillis >= 0) { "Expected non-negative delay, but has $delayMillis ms" }
 69â†’    require(initialDelayMillis >= 0) { "Expected non-negative initial delay, but has $initialDelayMillis ms" }
 70â†’    return GlobalScope.produce(Dispatchers.Unconfined + context, capacity = 0) {
 71â†’        when (mode) {
 72â†’            TickerMode.FIXED_PERIOD -> fixedPeriodTicker(delayMillis, initialDelayMillis, channel)
 73â†’            TickerMode.FIXED_DELAY -> fixedDelayTicker(delayMillis, initialDelayMillis, channel)
 74â†’        }
 75â†’    }
 76â†’}

 78â†’private suspend fun fixedPeriodTicker(
 79â†’    delayMillis: Long,
 80â†’    initialDelayMillis: Long,
 81â†’    channel: SendChannel<Unit>
 82â†’) {
 83â†’    var deadline = nanoTime() + delayToNanos(initialDelayMillis)
 84â†’    delay(initialDelayMillis)
 85â†’    val delayNs = delayToNanos(delayMillis)
 86â†’    while (true) {
 87â†’        deadline += delayNs
 88â†’        channel.send(Unit)
 89â†’        val now = nanoTime()
 90â†’        val nextDelay = (deadline - now).coerceAtLeast(0)
 91â†’        if (nextDelay == 0L && delayNs != 0L) {
 92â†’            val adjustedDelay = delayNs - (now - deadline) % delayNs
 93â†’            deadline = now + adjustedDelay
 94â†’            delay(delayNanosToMillis(adjustedDelay))
 95â†’        } else {
 96â†’            delay(delayNanosToMillis(nextDelay))
 97â†’        }
 98â†’    }
 99â†’}

 43â†’     *

 45â†’     * * `<= 0` -- there are potentially more events for immediate processing;
 46â†’     * * `> 0` -- a number of nanoseconds to wait for next scheduled event;
 47â†’     * * [Long.MAX_VALUE] -- no more events.

 49â†’     * **NOTE**: Must be invoked only from the event loop's thread
 50â†’     *          (no check for performance reasons, may be added in the future).
 51â†’     */

 53â†’        if (!processUnconfinedEvent()) return Long.MAX_VALUE
 54â†’        return 0
 55â†’    }

 58â†’

153â†’/**
154â†’ * First-line overflow protection -- limit maximal delay.
155â†’ * Delays longer than this one (~146 years) are considered to be delayed "forever".

159â†’internal fun delayToNanos(timeMillis: Long): Long = when {
160â†’    timeMillis <= 0 -> 0L
161â†’    timeMillis >= MAX_MS -> Long.MAX_VALUE
162â†’    else -> timeMillis * MS_TO_NS
163â†’}

165â†’internal fun delayNanosToMillis(timeNanos: Long): Long =
166â†’    timeNanos / MS_TO_NS

331â†’                    if (result !== Queue.REMOVE_FROZEN) return result as Runnable?
332â†’                    _queue.compareAndSet(queue, queue.next())
333â†’                }
334â†’                else -> when {

340â†’    }
